name: Pydantic Logfire
what_it_does: Pydantic Logfire is an OpenTelemetry-based observability platform by the Pydantic team with first-class Python integration. It provides a simple Python SDK with automatic instrumentation for FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, HTTPX, OpenAI SDK, and other Python libraries, exporting traces and logs to the Logfire cloud dashboard.
use_cases: Adding structured observability to Python-based agents with minimal boilerplate via logfire.instrument_*() helpers
Tracing LLM calls through the OpenAI SDK with automatic token counting and latency tracking
Instrumenting FastAPI-based agent APIs with automatic request/response tracing
Correlating database queries (via SQLAlchemy instrumentation) with the agent reasoning traces that triggered them
Using logfire.span() context managers to create semantic spans around agent reasoning steps
Shipping structured Python log records alongside OTel traces in a unified dashboard
not_for: Non-Python agent stacks — Logfire SDK is Python-only
Teams that need to self-host their observability backend — Logfire cloud is required for the dashboard
Production workloads with strict data residency requirements outside US/EU
Very high-volume telemetry pipelines where log retention cost becomes significant
best_when: You are building Python agents using Pydantic, FastAPI, or the OpenAI SDK and want immediate, low-friction observability with deep framework integration and an opinionated but clean dashboard.
avoid_when: Your agent stack is not Python, you need a self-hosted observability backend, or you need deeper infrastructure metrics beyond application-level tracing and logging.

auth.notes: A Logfire token (write key) is configured in the SDK via logfire.configure(token='...') or LOGFIRE_TOKEN environment variable. Tokens are project-scoped. Dashboard access uses email/OAuth login. No fine-grained scope control on write tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 30-day log retention, up to 1M spans/month, 10GB storage; unlimited team members on free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Scale and Enterprise tiers available with longer retention, higher volume, and SLAs — pricing available on request
pricing.notes: Free tier is genuinely generous for individual developers and small teams. 30-day retention covers most debugging workflows. The team is Pydantic-backed and growing — pricing may evolve.

performance.rate_limits: Free tier: 1M spans/month. SDK uses async background export — minimal impact on application latency.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Logfire SDK exceptions are surfaced as Python exceptions with clear messages. OTLP export failures are logged to stderr but do not crash the application. Configuration errors (bad token, wrong endpoint) report clearly at startup.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Telemetry is write-once, fire-and-forget. No deduplication. Retries on network failure may produce duplicate spans.
known_agent_gotchas: logfire.configure() must be called before any other instrumentation — call it at module import time
Automatic OpenAI instrumentation via logfire.instrument_openai() captures prompts and completions — ensure PII handling is considered
SDK is Python-only — no direct integration for agents written in other languages
Dashboard is cloud-only — no self-hosted option for the Logfire UI
Span names default to function/method names — add explicit span names for meaningful traces in complex agent loops
Token must be set before any spans are created or they will be dropped silently

security_score_components.security_notes: LOGFIRE_TOKEN should be treated as a write credential — anyone with it can send data to your project. Logfire cloud enforces TLS. Pydantic team maintains clean dependency hygiene. LLM prompt data may be captured in spans — sanitize sensitive inputs before tracing.
